evidence_quotes.quote: remove any trace of a video game and its availability for playing.

paragraphs.text: Hi, As a citizen of EU, I am greatly pleased with the initiative behind this Digital Fairness Act. It is high time that EU becomes a role model for regulating the digital space so that it is conducive to a productive digital space. However, I would suggest that the Digital Fairness Act also addresses skewed consumer practices within video games, particularly so-called "live service" games, where a publisher is able to remove any trace of a video game and its availability for playing. Not only is this terrible from a consumer practice, as a consumer, I surely must be able to enjoy the products I have purchased, but it is also a loss for the arts, as these artifacts must be considered for their historical value. With EU taking the lead in consumer rights, such an addendum within the Digital Fairness Act would simply represent further cohesion with other pro-consumer laws enacted within the EU. Thank you for taking the time to read my feedback!
